description: Test cases for affiliate-skills. Each case has an input prompt, expected patterns in output, and pass criteria. Run with scripts/run-evals.sh.

tests.name: Category search with filters
tests.input_prompt: I want to promote AI video tools, commission recurring, at least 20%
tests.pass_criteria: Output contains a comparison table with at least 2 programs, a scored recommendation with all 5 dimensions, and actionable next steps. Programs shown must have recurring commission ≥20%.

tests.name: Head-to-head comparison
tests.input_prompt: Compare HeyGen vs Synthesia for my LinkedIn audience
tests.pass_criteria: Output contains side-by-side comparison of both programs with scores. Content Potential and platform fit for LinkedIn are explicitly discussed. Clear winner recommendation.

tests.name: Beginner with no criteria
tests.input_prompt: I'm new to affiliate marketing. What should I promote?
tests.pass_criteria: Output defaults to beginner-friendly criteria (AI/SaaS, recurring, easy to demo). Recommends products with free tiers and low payout thresholds. Includes clear next steps.

tests.name: Recurring commission projection
tests.input_prompt: Calculate my earnings if I promote HeyGen (30% recurring, $48/mo product) and get 500 visitors/month to my blog
tests.pass_criteria: Output shows monthly projections with compounding for recurring commissions. Includes month 1, month 3, and month 12 estimates. Shows conversion rate assumptions and cumulative earnings.

tests.name: Goal-based calculation
tests.input_prompt: How many sales do I need to make $1000/month with Semrush ($200 per sale)?
tests.pass_criteria: Output calculates 5 sales needed. Shows traffic required at different conversion rates. Includes realistic timeline estimate.

tests.name: Analyze competitor site
tests.input_prompt: What affiliate programs does authorityhacker.com promote? What can I learn from their strategy?
tests.pass_criteria: Output identifies likely affiliate programs with confidence levels. Analyzes content formats and SEO approach. Provides replicability score and content gaps.

tests.name: Niche competitor landscape
tests.input_prompt: Who are the top affiliate sites in the AI tools niche?
tests.pass_criteria: Output identifies 3+ competitor sites with analysis. Shows content gaps and opportunities. Recommends programs to focus on based on competitor analysis.

tests.name: Beginner niche selection
tests.input_prompt: I want to start affiliate marketing but don't know what niche to pick. I like technology and productivity.
tests.pass_criteria: Output scores at least 3 niche candidates. Each has demand, competition, and program availability scores. Includes content angle suggestions. Clear recommendation for beginner.

tests.name: Saturated niche pivot
tests.input_prompt: Is 'web hosting' still a good affiliate niche or is it too saturated?
tests.pass_criteria: Output honestly assesses competition level. Suggests micro-niche angles within hosting or adjacent niches. Shows data-backed reasoning for recommendations.

tests.name: LinkedIn post for a specific product
tests.input_prompt: Write a LinkedIn post promoting HeyGen
tests.pass_criteria: Output contains a ready-to-copy LinkedIn post with a clear hook in the first line, no external link in the post body (link in comments), FTC disclosure, and posting tips. Must NOT start with 'I'm excited to share'.

tests.name: X thread for SEO tool
tests.input_prompt: Create an X thread about Semrush for SEO marketers
tests.pass_criteria: Output contains a multi-tweet thread (5+ tweets), numbered or clearly separated. First tweet has a compelling hook. Last tweet contains CTA, link, and #ad disclosure. Content is specific to SEO use cases.

tests.name: Reddit post with personal experience
tests.input_prompt: I've been using Notion for 2 years, help me write a Reddit post
tests.pass_criteria: Output includes both pros and cons (Reddit requires honesty). Uses the user's personal experience. Includes 'Full disclosure: affiliate link' at the bottom. Suggests relevant subreddits. Does NOT read like an ad.

tests.name: All platforms for a single product
tests.input_prompt: Promote GetResponse on all platforms
tests.pass_criteria: Output contains at least 3 separate posts for different platforms (LinkedIn, X, Reddit). Each post is formatted differently and tailored to the platform. Each has appropriate FTC disclosure for that platform.

tests.name: Genuine recommendation post
tests.input_prompt: Write a Reddit post for r/productivity recommending Notion as a project management tool
tests.pass_criteria: Output is subreddit-appropriate tone. Includes genuine pros AND cons. Affiliate link in comments not body. FTC disclosure present. At least one product limitation mentioned.

tests.name: Comment reply format
tests.input_prompt: Someone in r/Entrepreneur asked 'What tools do you use for email marketing?' — write a reply recommending GetResponse
tests.pass_criteria: Output is a comment reply (not a standalone post). Adds value beyond just recommending the product. Mentions real features. FTC disclosure at end. Casual, helpful tone.

tests.name: Product demo script
tests.input_prompt: Write a 45-second TikTok script demoing HeyGen's AI avatar feature
tests.pass_criteria: Output has scene-by-scene breakdown with timecodes. Sentences under 10 words. Text overlay on every scene. FTC '#ad' as text overlay (not just caption). Hook in first 3 seconds.

tests.name: Before/after script
tests.input_prompt: TikTok script: before and after using Canva Pro for my small business marketing
tests.pass_criteria: Output follows before/after framework. Clear visual contrast. Specific results shown. Under 60 seconds. Sound-optional (text overlays tell the story).

tests.name: Educational thread
tests.input_prompt: Write an X thread teaching 5 SEO tips using Semrush, targeted at freelancers
tests.pass_criteria: Thread has 7+ tweets. Each tweet under 280 chars. Product mention at 60-70% through. #ad in CTA tweet. Educational value independent of the product pitch.

tests.name: Hot take thread
tests.input_prompt: Write a contrarian X thread about why most people use the wrong video tools, then recommend HeyGen
tests.pass_criteria: Hook tweet is bold/contrarian. Thread builds argument before introducing product. Product feels like natural conclusion not forced plug. #ad disclosed.

tests.name: Product review article
tests.input_prompt: Write a review blog post for HeyGen targeting content creators
tests.pass_criteria: Full article with SEO metadata, pros/cons, pricing section, verdict with CTA, FTC disclosure near top, 2000+ words. Meta description 150-160 chars.

tests.name: Comparison blog post
tests.input_prompt: Write a blog post comparing HeyGen vs Synthesia for business video creation
tests.pass_criteria: Output has comparison table with 6+ dimensions, ✅/❌ indicators, clear winner in verdict, FTC disclosure, both products get fair coverage.

tests.name: Head-to-head comparison
tests.input_prompt: Write a 'Semrush vs Ahrefs' comparison blog post for SEO beginners
tests.pass_criteria: Feature comparison table with 6-10 dimensions. ✅/❌/⚠️ used. Both products get fair, balanced treatment. Clear winner in verdict. FTC disclosure above fold. 2500+ words.

tests.name: Three-way comparison
tests.input_prompt: Compare Notion vs ClickUp vs Monday.com for project management — which should I promote as an affiliate?
tests.pass_criteria: Comparison covers all 3 products. Affiliate angle considered (which has best commission). Comparison table present. Clear recommendation with reasoning.

tests.name: Step-by-step tutorial
tests.input_prompt: Write a tutorial: How to create your first AI video with HeyGen (step by step for beginners)
tests.pass_criteria: Numbered steps (not bulleted). Prerequisites section present. FTC disclosure after title. Each step is atomic (one action). No invented UI details. CTA doesn't interrupt steps.

tests.name: Problem-solution tutorial
tests.input_prompt: Write a how-to guide: How to automate your social media content creation using AI tools
tests.pass_criteria: Problem stated clearly in intro. Steps lead to solution. Product recommendation feels natural. Word count 2000+. Includes 'what you'll need' section.

tests.name: Top tools listicle
tests.input_prompt: Write a 'Top 7 AI Video Tools in 2026' listicle article for my blog
tests.pass_criteria: Title has year. At a Glance table present. 7 entries with pros/cons/pricing/best-for. Primary affiliate ranked #1-3. FTC disclosure before entries. 3000+ words.

tests.name: Niche listicle
tests.input_prompt: Write a 'Best 5 Email Marketing Tools for Small Businesses' listicle
tests.pass_criteria: 5 products with mini-reviews. Pricing tables per entry. Comparison matrix at end. 'How to Choose' section present. Each entry has clear CTA.

tests.name: Single product landing page
tests.input_prompt: Create a landing page for HeyGen (30% recurring, AI video tool)
tests.pass_criteria: Self-contained HTML file. Has viewport meta tag. 3+ CTAs. FTC disclosure before first affiliate link. No external resources. Affitor footer. Mobile-responsive.

tests.name: Comparison landing page
tests.input_prompt: Create a comparison landing page: HeyGen vs Synthesia — which is better for marketers?
tests.pass_criteria: HTML with comparison table. Side-by-side layout. Clear winner recommendation. Both products have CTAs. Self-contained, no external resources.

tests.name: Deep dive showcase
tests.input_prompt: Build a product showcase page for Semrush — highlight SEO, content marketing, and competitive analysis features
tests.pass_criteria: Self-contained HTML. Feature sections for each capability. Pricing breakdown. 4+ CTAs. Testimonials section. noindex meta tag. Affitor footer.

tests.name: SaaS showcase with free tier
tests.input_prompt: Create a showcase page for Notion highlighting the free plan as entry point
tests.pass_criteria: Free plan emphasized as low-friction entry. Feature comparison between free and paid. Self-contained HTML. Mobile-responsive.

tests.name: Lead magnet squeeze page
tests.input_prompt: Build a squeeze page offering a free 'AI Video Toolkit' checklist, then redirect to HeyGen affiliate link
tests.pass_criteria: Self-contained HTML. Single email field. No navigation links. Lead magnet mockup present. Form redirects to affiliate URL. FTC disclosure in footer. noindex meta tag. Privacy micro-copy.

tests.name: Minimal squeeze page
tests.input_prompt: Create a simple opt-in page for my SEO tips newsletter with Semrush as the affiliate backend
tests.pass_criteria: Minimal, focused design. One clear value proposition. Email capture form. Self-contained HTML. Mobile-first.

tests.name: Live webinar page
tests.input_prompt: Build a registration page for my webinar 'Master AI Video in 60 Minutes' on April 15 2026 at 7pm EST, featuring HeyGen
tests.pass_criteria: Self-contained HTML. Countdown timer with correct date. Registration form (name + email). Speaker section. Agenda. FTC disclosure. Urgency bar. Affitor footer.

tests.name: Evergreen webinar page
tests.input_prompt: Create an evergreen webinar registration page for 'How to Start Affiliate Marketing' — always available
tests.pass_criteria: Evergreen countdown (resets for each visitor). No specific date. Registration form. Self-contained HTML. Mobile-responsive.

tests.name: Affiliate bio link page
tests.input_prompt: Create a bio link page for @techreviewer with links to my HeyGen review blog, Semrush landing page, YouTube channel, and Twitter profile
tests.pass_criteria: Self-contained HTML. All 4 links present with labels. Mobile-first layout. Money links ordered first. FTC disclosure if affiliate links. Affitor footer.

tests.name: Themed bio link
tests.input_prompt: Make me a dark theme bio link hub for @aimarketer with 5 affiliate product links and my social profiles
tests.pass_criteria: Dark theme CSS applied. All links categorized. Affiliate links grouped separately from social. Self-contained HTML. Mobile-optimized.

tests.name: Welcome sequence for affiliate
tests.input_prompt: Write a 5-email welcome sequence for new subscribers promoting HeyGen as the main affiliate product
tests.pass_criteria: 5 emails with subject lines, preview text, full body, and CTAs. FTC disclosure on affiliate emails. Value emails outnumber pitch emails. Spacing between emails is logical.

tests.name: Product launch drip
tests.input_prompt: Create a 7-email drip sequence for promoting Semrush to an SEO-focused email list
tests.pass_criteria: 7 emails with progressive engagement (value → trust → offer). Each email has subject + body + CTA. FTC compliant. Includes setup notes for ESP.

tests.name: Deploy landing page
tests.input_prompt: Help me deploy my HeyGen landing page HTML file to GitHub Pages with a custom domain myaffiliatesite.com
tests.pass_criteria: Step-by-step deployment guide. GitHub Actions workflow or manual steps. CNAME file for custom domain. DNS record instructions. All commands copy-paste ready.

tests.name: Simple deploy without custom domain
tests.input_prompt: Deploy my bio link page to GitHub Pages for free — no custom domain needed
tests.pass_criteria: Simplified guide without custom domain. Repository setup steps. Expected URL format (username.github.io/repo). README included.

tests.name: 30-day content calendar
tests.input_prompt: Create a 30-day social media calendar for promoting HeyGen on LinkedIn and Twitter
tests.pass_criteria: 30 posts across 4 weeks. 80/20 value-to-promo ratio. Platform-specific formatting. FTC disclosure on promo posts. Posting times included.

tests.name: Multi-product calendar
tests.input_prompt: Plan my social media for next month promoting HeyGen, Semrush, and Notion across LinkedIn and X
tests.pass_criteria: All 3 products represented. Posts balanced across programs. Value content between promo posts. Platform-adapted formatting.

tests.name: Basic tracking setup
tests.input_prompt: Set up tracking for my HeyGen affiliate link (heygen.com/ref/abc123) on blog and Twitter
tests.pass_criteria: Output contains UTM-tagged links for both blog and Twitter. Each link has proper utm_source, utm_medium, and utm_campaign parameters. Includes a naming convention and setup guide.

tests.name: Multi-platform campaign
tests.input_prompt: Create tracking links for Semrush across LinkedIn, Twitter, Reddit, blog, and email
tests.pass_criteria: Output contains at least 5 distinct UTM-tagged links, one per platform. Naming convention is consistent. Includes tracking setup guide.

tests.name: Headline A/B test
tests.input_prompt: Test this headline: 'HeyGen Review: Is It Worth It in 2026?'
tests.pass_criteria: Output contains the original headline plus at least 3 variants. Each variant has a hypothesis explaining why it might outperform. Includes a test plan with sample size and duration.

tests.name: CTA optimization
tests.input_prompt: Optimize this CTA: 'Start Free Trial'
tests.pass_criteria: Output contains at least 3 CTA alternatives with different approaches (outcome-focused, friction-reducing, urgency). Each has a hypothesis. Test plan included.

tests.name: Multi-program monthly report
tests.input_prompt: Monthly report: HeyGen 500 clicks 15 conversions $450, Semrush 1200 clicks 8 conversions $320, Notion 300 clicks 25 conversions $125
tests.pass_criteria: Output contains KPI calculations (EPC, conversion rate) for each program. Programs are ranked. Labels assigned (Star, Cash Cow, etc.). Actionable recommendations provided.

tests.name: Blog post SEO audit
tests.input_prompt: Audit this blog post for 'best AI video tools': [sample 500-word review without meta description or keyword in H1]
tests.pass_criteria: Output contains a scorecard with at least 8 dimensions scored 1-10. Identifies missing meta description and keyword-less H1 as issues. Includes a prioritized fix-it checklist with quick wins.

tests.name: Welcome flow with branches
tests.input_prompt: Build a welcome email automation for HeyGen for content creators who downloaded my AI guide
tests.pass_criteria: Output contains a multi-email flow with at least one branch point (opened/not opened). Each email has subject, preview, body. FTC disclosure present on emails with affiliate links. Flow diagram included.

tests.name: Blog to social media
tests.input_prompt: Turn my HeyGen review blog post into a tweet thread and LinkedIn post
tests.pass_criteria: Output contains at least 2 distinct formats (tweet thread and LinkedIn post). Each is adapted to the platform's style and length. FTC disclosure present on both. Posting tips included.

tests.name: Portfolio analysis with clear winner
tests.input_prompt: I promote HeyGen ($450/mo), Semrush ($320/mo), Notion ($125/mo), Canva ($80/mo). Which should I focus on?
tests.pass_criteria: Output contains portfolio overview with revenue share percentages. Each program gets an action label. Strategic recommendations with specific next steps. Weekly time allocation plan.

tests.name: Facebook ad for SaaS
tests.input_prompt: Write Facebook ads for HeyGen targeting content creators
tests.pass_criteria: Output contains at least 3 ad variants with different angles. Each has primary text, headline, description, CTA. Facebook-specific compliance notes included. Targeting suggestions provided.

tests.name: Task-based search
tests.input_prompt: I want to write a blog review of an AI tool
tests.pass_criteria: Output recommends affiliate-blog-builder as top match. Shows at least 2 related skills. Includes example invocation prompt. Suggests a path if applicable.

tests.name: New user orientation
tests.input_prompt: I'm new to Affitor, where do I start?
tests.pass_criteria: Output shows available stages and recommends a starting path. Mentions S1 research as the typical starting point. Includes at least 3 skills with descriptions.

tests.name: Beginner full funnel plan
tests.input_prompt: I want to start affiliate marketing. I have 5 hours a week and I blog.
tests.pass_criteria: Output contains a week-by-week roadmap. References specific skills by name. Includes invocation prompts. Has success metrics. Fits within 5 hours/week constraint.

tests.name: Missing disclosure detection
tests.input_prompt: Check this tweet: 'Just tried HeyGen and it's incredible. Use my link: heygen.com/ref/abc123'
tests.pass_criteria: Output identifies missing FTC disclosure as a critical issue. Provides corrected content with #ad added. Scores compliance as FAIL. Explains the FTC requirement.

tests.name: Low conversion diagnosis
tests.input_prompt: I wrote 3 blog reviews last month. 2000 visitors but only 2 conversions ($14 total). What went wrong?
tests.pass_criteria: Output compares 0.1% conversion rate to industry benchmark (1-3%). Identifies root causes. Provides prioritized improvement actions with specific skill references. Includes an iteration plan.
